title: Building using the docker image *without* privileged mode
body:
Hi,

I'm unable to build using the official image here - https://quay.io/repository/containers/buildah - without using the --privileged flag. Does buildah work without having root/system-level privileges ?

$ docker run --rm -it quay.io/containers/buildah:latest bash
Unable to find image 'quay.io/containers/buildah:latest' locally
latest: Pulling from containers/buildah
4545346f2a49: Pull complete 
a14e2cbf1395: Pull complete 
45f226666774: Pull complete 
d9b379f55b51: Pull complete 
1c43bac99d23: Pull complete 
9defd66bd597: Pull complete 
Digest: sha256:478a131c9f61168b039607ee5e8290fcac2119200ea61a61bfc9dd9f1eb956f1
Status: Downloaded newer image for quay.io/containers/buildah:latest
[root@fe92df20d1f2 /]# buildah from alpine
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Operation not permitted
ERRO[0000] error parsing PID "": strconv.Atoi: parsing "": invalid syntax 
ERRO[0000] (unable to determine exit status)            
[root@fe92df20d1f2 /]# exit

$ docker version
Client: Docker Engine - Community
 Version:           20.10.11
 API version:       1.41

  Version:          0.19.0
  GitCommit:        de40ad0

comments.body:
>  Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Operation not permitted

you need to allow nested containers
But to allow nested containers means that I will need to run buildah with the --privileged flag. Is there a unprivileged/rootless way to build images using buildah ?

I talk a lot about this in this article.
https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-inside-container
This is about podman build but buildah has similar features.
